message:
feat(types): Add scope utilities, bullet ID validation pattern

NEW SCOPE UTILITIES (src/types.ts):
- ScopeContext interface: workspace, language, framework, task
- isBulletInScope(bullet, ctx): Check if bullet applies to context
- filterBulletsByScope(bullets, ctx): Filter bullets by scope context

Scope matching logic:
- global: Always in scope
- workspace: Match bullet.workspace to ctx.workspace
- language: Match bullet.scopeKey to ctx.language (case-insensitive)
- framework: Match bullet.scopeKey to ctx.framework (case-insensitive)
- task: Match bullet.scopeKey to ctx.task (case-insensitive)

BULLET ID VALIDATION:
- Add BULLET_ID_PATTERN regex: /^b-[a-z0-9]+-[a-z0-9]+$/
- Validate bullet IDs match format "b-{timestamp36}-{random}"
- Ensures consistent ID generation and early detection of malformed data

TEST CLEANUP:
- test/format-last-helpful.test.ts: Remove unused beforeAll/afterAll imports
- test/format-raw-session.test.ts: New tests for formatRawSession

BEADS:
- Update issue tracking state

WHY:
Scope filtering is essential for retrieving only relevant rules in specific
contexts (e.g., TypeScript-specific rules for TS files). The ID validation
pattern catches data corruption early.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-vol","title":"[Types] HarmfulReason - Structured reasons for harmful marks","description":"## Overview
Define enumeration of structured reasons for marking a bullet as 'harmful'. Enables analysis of WHY rules fail, not just THAT they fail.

## Purpose
When a playbook rule causes problems, capturing the specific failure mode enables:
1. Pattern detection - Identify systemic issues (e.g., 'outdated rules are most common problem')
2. Targeted improvements - Address root causes (e.g., add validation for outdated patterns)
3. Better refinement - Reflector can learn from failure categories
4. User insights - Help users understand what went wrong

## Enumeration Values

### caused_bug
- Definition: Following the rule directly introduced a defect or error
- Example: 'Use AuthService.login()' but AuthService was refactored to AuthManager
- Severity: HIGH - broke functionality
- Action: Often requires immediate deprecation or replacement

### wasted_time
- Definition: Advice led to unnecessary work or dead ends
- Example: 'Run full test suite before commit' when project has no tests
- Severity: MEDIUM - productivity loss
- Action: Refine rule to check preconditions

### contradicted_requirements
- Definition: Rule conflicted with actual user needs or project goals
- Example: 'Use REST API' when project mandate is GraphQL
- Severity: MEDIUM - misalignment
- Action: Update rule or mark as wrong scope

### wrong_context
- Definition: Rule is valid but was applied in inappropriate situation
- Example: 'Use React hooks' suggested in Vue.js project
- Severity: LOW - scope issue
- Action: Improve scope filtering (workspace/framework)

### outdated
- Definition: Rule was correct historically but no longer applicable
- Example: 'Use class components' after project migrated to hooks
- Severity: MEDIUM - temporal drift
- Action: Add verification check or automatic expiration

### other
- Definition: Catchall for unclassified failures
- Example: Edge cases not fitting above categories
- Severity: VARIABLE
- Action: Review and potentially add new category

## Type Definition
type HarmfulReason = 'caused_bug' | 'wasted_time' | 'contradicted_requirements' | 'wrong_context' | 'outdated' | 'other'

## Usage in FeedbackEvent
const harmfulEvent: FeedbackEvent = { timestamp: '2025-12-07T14:30:00Z', sessionPath: '/path/to/session.jsonl', reason: 'caused_bug', context: 'AuthService was refactored to AuthManager' }

## Analytics Use Cases
- Stats command: Show distribution of failure reasons
- Audit command: Group violations by reason
- Reflector: Learn patterns (e.g., 'outdated' rules need verification)
- Auto-prune: Higher weight for 'caused_bug' vs 'wrong_context'

## Acceptance Criteria
- TypeScript type with 6 enum values
- JSDoc for each value explaining semantics
- Examples of each failure mode in docs
- Integration with FeedbackEvent type
- Used in harmful event creation flows","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:57.133573-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T19:02:10.081706-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T19:02:00.164172-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-vuxg","title":"[Config] detectRepoContext - Find .cass/ directory","description":"Function signature:
```typescript
async function detectRepoContext(): Promise<{
  inRepo: boolean;
  repoRoot?: string;
  cassDir?: string;
}>
```

Implementation:
1. Run `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` to find repo root
   - If fails: not in a git repo, return { inRepo: false }
2. Check if .cass/ directory exists in repo root
3. Return context information

Return values:
- inRepo: true if in a git repository
- repoRoot: absolute path to git repo root (if inRepo)
- cassDir: absolute path to .cass/ directory (if exists)

Used by config loader to determine if repo-level config should be loaded.
Called at startup of most commands to establish context.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:54.839482-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:54.839482-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-w4g","title":"[Utils] extractKeywords - Stop word filtered keyword extraction","description":"","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:13.74917-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:13.74917-05:00","comments":[{"id":33,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-w4g","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function extractKeywords(text: string): string[]
```

## Implementation Details
- Tokenize text using tokenize() helper
- Define stop words list (~80 common words):
  - Articles: the, a, an
  - Verbs: is, are, was, were, be, been, have, has, had, do, does, did, will, would, could, should, can
  - Prepositions: to, of, in, for, on, with, at, by, from, as, into, through, during, before, after
  - Pronouns: I, you, he, she, it, we, they, me, him, her, us, them
  - Conjunctions: and, or, but, if, when, where, why, how
  - Other: this, that, these, those, what, which, who, there, here
- Filter out stop words: tokens.filter(t => !stopWords.includes(t))
- Return top 10 keywords by frequency (or all if fewer than 10)

## Why This Matters
Used for bullet scoring and cass query generation. Removing stop words focuses on meaningful terms. "Fix authentication bug" extracts ["fix", "authentication", "bug"] not ["fix", "the", "authentication", "bug"].

## Edge Cases to Handle
- All stop words - return []
- No stop words - return all tokens (up to 10)
- Fewer than 10 keywords - return all
- Duplicate keywords - count frequency, return unique tokens","created_at":"2025-12-07T23:15:50Z"}]}
-{"id":"cass_memory_system-w5xt","title":"[Diary] formatRawSession - Parse raw session formats","description":"Format raw session files when cass export is unavailable.

Function Signature:
function formatRawSession(content: string, ext: string): string

Supported Extensions and Formats:

1. Markdown (.md):
   - Return content as-is
   - Already human-readable
   - Used by aider

2. JSONL (.jsonl):
   - Parse each line as separate JSON object
   - Expected schema: { role: string, content: string }
   - Format as: "**{role}**: {content}\n\n"
   - Skip invalid lines with warning comment
   - Used by Claude

3. JSON (.json):
   - Parse as single JSON object
   - Expected schema: { messages: Array<{role, content}> }
   - Format each message as: "**{role}**: {content}\n\n"
   - Handle nested conversation structure
   - Used by various agents

Format Examples:

JSONL Input:
{"role":"user","content":"Hello"}
{"role":"assistant","content":"Hi there!"}

Formatted Output:
**user**: Hello

**assistant**: Hi there!

JSON Input:
{"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Hello"},{"role":"assistant","content":"Hi!"}]}

Formatted Output:
**user**: Hello

**assistant**: Hi!

Error Handling:
- Invalid JSON: Include raw line with [PARSE ERROR] prefix
- Missing fields: Use [unknown] for role or [empty] for content
- Unsupported extension: Return raw content with warning header

Key Features:
- Human-readable output
- Preserves conversation structure
- Graceful handling of malformed data
- Consistent formatting across file types

Output:
Returns formatted string ready for LLM extraction.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:47:33.384464-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:16:44.63905-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-w6c","title":"[Types] Delta Schema - Playbook Change Operations","description":"## Overview
Define Delta schema - a discriminated union of all possible playbook mutation operations. Enables type-safe, auditable changes with clear semantics.

## Purpose
Deltas provide:
1. Type-safe playbook modifications
2. Audit trail of changes
3. Rollback/undo capability (future)
4. Batch operations (apply multiple deltas)
5. Clear separation of concerns (one type per operation)

## Delta Types (Discriminated Union)

Use Zod's discriminatedUnion on 'type' field for type safety.

### 1. Add Delta
```typescript
{
  type: 'add',
  bullet: NewBulletData,
  reason: string,
  sourceSession: string
}
```

Purpose: Create new playbook bullet
Fields:
- bullet: Partial PlaybookBullet (id, createdAt, updatedAt auto-generated)
- reason: Why this rule is being added (for audit trail)
- sourceSession: Which session prompted this addition
Example: { type: 'add', bullet: { content: 'Use TokenValidator for JWT checks', category: 'authentication', scope: 'workspace', workspace: '/path/to/project' }, reason: 'Authentication bypass incident', sourceSession: '/cass/2025-12-07-auth-fix.jsonl' }

### 2. Helpful Delta
```typescript
{
  type: 'helpful',
  bulletId: string,
  sourceSession?: string
}
```

Purpose: Mark bullet as helpful (append to helpfulEvents)
Fields:
- bulletId: ID of bullet to mark helpful
- sourceSession: Optional session that benefited from rule
Effect: Appends FeedbackEvent to bullet.helpfulEvents with current timestamp
Example: { type: 'helpful', bulletId: 'b-l7k9m2p4-a3f8', sourceSession: '/cass/2025-12-07-api-design.jsonl' }

### 3. Harmful Delta
```typescript
{
  type: 'harmful',
  bulletId: string,
  sourceSession?: string,
  reason?: HarmfulReason
}
```

Purpose: Mark bullet as harmful (append to harmfulEvents)
Fields:
- bulletId: ID of bullet to mark harmful
- sourceSession: Optional session where rule caused problems
- reason: Optional HarmfulReason enum (caused_bug, outdated, etc.)
Effect: Appends FeedbackEvent to bullet.harmfulEvents with current timestamp
Auto-Trigger: If harmfulEvents.length >= config.pruneHarmfulThreshold, transition to retired state
Example: { type: 'harmful', bulletId: 'b-m8n3p5q7-b4g9', sourceSession: '/cass/2025-11-20-refactor.jsonl', reason: 'outdated' }

### 4. Replace Delta
```typescript
{
  type: 'replace',
  bulletId: string,
  newContent: string,
  reason: string
}
```

Purpose: Update bullet content (refinement)
Fields:
- bulletId: ID of bullet to update
- newContent: New rule text
- reason: Why content is being changed
Effect: Updates bullet.content and bullet.updatedAt
Audit: Creates history entry (future enhancement)
Example: { type: 'replace', bulletId: 'b-n9o4q6r8-c5h0', newContent: 'Use TokenValidator from @/lib/auth for all JWT validation', reason: 'Made rule more specific to this codebase' }

### 5. Deprecate Delta
```typescript
{
  type: 'deprecate',
  bulletId: string,
  reason: string,
  replacedBy?: string
}
```

Purpose: Retire bullet (mark as deprecated)
Fields:
- bulletId: ID of bullet to deprecate
- reason: Why rule is being retired
- replacedBy: Optional ID of replacement bullet
Effect: Sets bullet.state = 'retired', bullet.maturity = 'deprecated', bullet.retiredAt = now
Gemini Pattern: Adds to deprecatedPatterns list in playbook
Example: { type: 'deprecate', bulletId: 'b-o0p5r7s9-d6i1', reason: 'Project migrated from class components to hooks', replacedBy: 'b-p1q6s8t0-e7j2' }

### 6. Merge Delta
```typescript
{
  type: 'merge',
  bulletIds: string[],
  mergedContent: string,
  reason: string
}
```

Purpose: Combine duplicate/similar bullets into one
Fields:
- bulletIds: Array of bullet IDs to merge (2+)
- mergedContent: Combined rule text
- reason: Why bullets are being merged
Effect:
  - Creates new bullet with mergedContent
  - Aggregates helpfulEvents and harmfulEvents from all merged bullets
  - Deprecates all source bullets with replacedBy pointing to merged bullet
  - Preserves provenance (sourceSessions, sourceAgents)
Example: { type: 'merge', bulletIds: ['b-a1b2c3d4', 'b-e5f6g7h8'], mergedContent: 'Use vitest for unit tests and playwright for e2e tests', reason: 'Deduplicated similar testing rules' }

## Discriminated Union Schema

```typescript
const DeltaSchema = z.discriminatedUnion('type', [
  z.object({ type: z.literal('add'), bullet: NewBulletDataSchema, reason: z.string(), sourceSession: z.string() }),
  z.object({ type: z.literal('helpful'), bulletId: z.string(), sourceSession: z.string().optional() }),
  z.object({ type: z.literal('harmful'), bulletId: z.string(), sourceSession: z.string().optional(), reason: HarmfulReasonSchema.optional() }),
  z.object({ type: z.literal('replace'), bulletId: z.string(), newContent: z.string(), reason: z.string() }),
  z.object({ type: z.literal('deprecate'), bulletId: z.string(), reason: z.string(), replacedBy: z.string().optional() }),
  z.object({ type: z.literal('merge'), bulletIds: z.array(z.string()).min(2), mergedContent: z.string(), reason: z.string() })
])

type Delta = z.infer<typeof DeltaSchema>
```

## Usage Patterns

### Single Delta Application
```typescript
import { applyDelta } from './playbook'
const delta: Delta = { type: 'helpful', bulletId: 'b-abc123', sourceSession: '/cass/session.jsonl' }
const updatedPlaybook = await applyDelta(playbook, delta)
```

### Batch Delta Application
```typescript
const deltas: Delta[] = [
  { type: 'add', bullet: {...}, reason: '...', sourceSession: '...' },
  { type: 'helpful', bulletId: 'b-xyz789', sourceSession: '...' },
  { type: 'deprecate', bulletId: 'b-old123', reason: '...' }
]
const updatedPlaybook = await applyDeltas(playbook, deltas)
```

### Type-Safe Handling
```typescript
function handleDelta(delta: Delta) {
  switch (delta.type) {
    case 'add':
      return createBullet(delta.bullet, delta.reason)
    case 'helpful':
      return markHelpful(delta.bulletId, delta.sourceSession)
    case 'harmful':
      return markHarmful(delta.bulletId, delta.sourceSession, delta.reason)
    case 'replace':
      return updateContent(delta.bulletId, delta.newContent, delta.reason)
    case 'deprecate':
      return deprecateBullet(delta.bulletId, delta.reason, delta.replacedBy)
    case 'merge':
      return mergeBullets(delta.bulletIds, delta.mergedContent, delta.reason)
  }
}
```

## Audit Trail Integration

Each delta should be logged for audit purposes:
```typescript
{
  timestamp: '2025-12-07T14:30:00Z',
  actor: 'claude',
  delta: { type: 'add', bullet: {...}, reason: '...', sourceSession: '...' },
  result: 'success' | 'failure',
  error?: string
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Zod discriminated union schema with all 6 delta types
- Type-safe TypeScript interfaces
- applyDelta function for single operations
- applyDeltas function for batch operations
- Type guard utilities for each delta type
- Examples for all delta types
- JSDoc documentation
- Audit trail integration hooks
- Validation for required fields
- Error handling for invalid deltas","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:03.277644-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:06:29.96717-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:05:24.099995-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-wco","title":"[Reflector] reflectOnSession - Main reflection function","description":"async function reflectOnSession(
  diary: DiaryEntry,
  sessionContent: string,
  existingBullets: PlaybookBullet[],
  config: Config
): Promise<PlaybookDelta[]>

Multi-iteration reflection (ACE pattern):
1. Initialize empty deltas array and seen hash set
2. For i = 0 to config.maxReflectorIterations:
   - Build prompt with iteration context
   - Call LLM for deltas
   - Deduplicate against seen hashes
   - Add unique deltas to array
   - If no new deltas, break early
3. Return all deltas

WHY MULTI-ITERATION: First pass catches obvious insights. Subsequent passes catch nuances that might be missed.

DEPENDENCIES:
- buildReflectorPrompt()
- deduplicateDeltas()
- validateReflectorOutput()
- shouldExitEarly()

OUTPUT: Array of PlaybookDelta objects ready for application to playbook","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:51.615811-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:56:09.918682-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:56:03.109249-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-wgf","title":"[Cass] cassTimeline - Get session activity over time","description":"Implement cassTimeline() to get session activity over time.

## Signature
```typescript
interface CassTimelineGroup {
  date: string;
  sessions: Array<{
    path: string;
    agent: string;
    messageCount: number;
    startTime: string;
    endTime: string;
  }>;
}

interface CassTimelineResult {
  groups: CassTimelineGroup[];
}

async function cassTimeline(
  days: number, 
  cassPath = "cass"
): Promise<CassTimelineResult>
```

## CLI Command
`cass timeline --days {days} --json`

## Implementation
- Use execFile() to run timeline command
- Parse JSON output
- Return structured timeline data
- Return { groups: [] } on error

## Error Handling
- Return empty groups array on any error
- Log parse errors at debug level
- No retries needed (timeline is read-only)

## Output Structure
Timeline groups sessions by date:
```json
{
  "groups": [
    {
      "date": "2024-01-15",
      "sessions": [
        {
          "path": "/path/to/session.txt",
          "agent": "claude-code",
          "messageCount": 42,
          "startTime": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
          "endTime": "2024-01-15T11:45:00Z"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

## Usage
Used for:
- Finding unprocessed sessions (reflect command)
- Activity visualization
- Stats and analytics
- Session discovery

```typescript
const timeline = await cassTimeline(7);
const allSessions = timeline.groups.flatMap(g => g.sessions);
```","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:00.092946-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T19:57:45.730685-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T19:57:42.548458-05:00"}
